news.t: Oracle's Cloud Boom Bolsters the Case for OGIG ETF
Tech Shift Fuels Double-Digit ETF Gains
OGIG Surges 8.4% on Oracle, DoorDash Gains
ALPS | O'Shares Exchange-Traded Funds to Transfer Listing to NYSE Arca
These three themes are driving the ETF industry in a volatile year, says head of iShares Americas

holdings.infoBox: OGIG is an equity ETF with a total of 55 individual holdings. The top holdings are Alphabet stock at 6.89%, Amazon.com at 6.65%, Microsoft at 5.64%, Meta Platforms at 5.27%, and Oracle at 4.87%.

overview.description: The ALPS O'Shares Global Internet Giants ETF (OGIG) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the O'Shares Global Internet Giants index. The fund tracks an index of global internet and internet technology stocks, selected and weighted by growth and quality factors. OGIG was launched on Jun 5, 2018 and is issued by SS&C.

data.title: These three themes are driving the ETF industry in a volatile year, says head of iShares Americas
data.text: Fixed income ETFs, sustainable investing and risk balancing have all become popular in 2020 as investors fielded a difficult market landscape, says Armando Senra, BlackRock's head of iShares Americas.

data.title: The year in ETFs: How the industry's faring in a volatile 2020
data.text: Armando Senra, head of iShares Americas at BlackRock, and Kevin O'Leary, chairman of O'Shares ETFs, assess the state of the exchange-traded fund industry with CNBC's Bob Pisani.
